description: An ablation changes prompt, model, and retrieval together, so the score delta cannot be attributed to any single factor.
body: Failure mode: several factors change in one comparison and a gain is credited to the intended change, but a confound (or an offsetting regression) is hidden inside the bundle, making the result uninterpretable. Detection signals: more than one variable differs between arms; dataset, metric, or judge version also changed; no recorded config diff. Do not bundle factors and credit one. Safer fallback: change exactly one factor per arm, hold dataset, metric, and judge fixed, record the config diff per arm, and label any multi-variable comparison inconclusive until isolated.
